task_completion.description: Open the BFI25.csv dataset in Jamovi
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi application window is open and displays the BFI25 dataset with columns A1 through A5 visible in the spreadsheet view.
In the Reliability Analysis setup panel, item A1 is placed in the 'Reverse-Scaled Items' box, or there is evidence of a newly computed/transformed variable where A1 is inverted (e.g., 7 - A1).

task_completion.description: Perform Reliability Analysis on the five Agreeableness items
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi results panel shows a 'Scale Reliability Statistics' table containing Cronbach's alpha for the items A1 (reversed), A2, A3, A4, and A5.

task_completion.description: Include 'Cronbach's alpha if item dropped' in the analysis
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi results panel displays an 'Item Reliability Statistics' table with a column specifically showing 'Cronbach's alpha' for each item if it is dropped.

task_completion.description: Save the Jamovi project to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi window title bar shows 'Agreeableness_Reliability.omv', or a file manager/terminal window shows the file 'Agreeableness_Reliability.omv' successfully saved in /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/.

task_completion.description: Create a text report with the correct reliability values
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the contents of 'reliability_report.txt' containing the overall alpha (0.716), the highest possible alpha if dropped (0.731), and the item to drop (A1).

integrity.description: The agent used the Jamovi graphical interface to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Jamovi GUI being interacted with (menus, variable selection, results panel) rather than a terminal running an R or Python script to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent extracted the correct values from the actual analysis rather than hardcoding them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Jamovi results panel should clearly display the values 0.716 and 0.731 before or while the agent writes them into the text report.

integrity.description: The agent created the text report in the correct directory using standard tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano) or standard terminal commands (echo, cat) are visible creating the file at /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/reliability_report.txt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct overall Cronbach's alpha for the Agreeableness scale (with A1 reverse-scored) is 0.716. The maximum possible alpha if one item is dropped is 0.731, which is achieved by dropping item A1. The final text report must contain these exact values (0.716, 0.731, A1).
